TextENGNew: ``...Is Houjou-san absent again today?@k@vS03/22/141400002. Furude-san, do you know anything about it?``
There was no reproach in the teacher's tone, but... watching Rika-chan hang her head, unable to answer, filled me with pity...
The teacher called Rika-chan up to the front and the two exchanged words in hushed voices at the lectern.
Rika-chan had initially said that Satoko had come down with a bad cold, but the entire class knew it was a lie.
...Because... every day, Rika-chan had been bringing Satoko's bento to school with her.
``......Satoko-chan...... what could have happened to her?``
``How should I know...``
......The one who wanted to know most of all... was none other than me.
This was already the third day Satoko had been absent.
Ever since that day... this weight on my heart refused to lift.
Just last Sunday, we had been having such a wonderful time together......
Satoko and I had bonded like real siblings... spending quiet but warm, unhurried hours together.
...So why had everything been... turned upside down... so suddenly...?
The more I dwelled on it...
......the more it seemed like the world had gone dark the moment Ooishi appeared.
Looking back now... perhaps that strange unease gnawing at me the night before Ooishi arrived had been an omen...
...Kuraudo Ooishi... Oyashiro-sama's emissary... huh...
``......Damn it, that's ridiculous...@k@vS03/01/140100003. I refuse to accept that Satoko's been chosen as this year's curse victim just because that guy showed up...``
What fault of mine... could have caused those happy times to end so abruptly?
...It was the day before Ooishi appeared. That much was certain.
Shion, Mion, and Rena had told me about the curse surrounding Satoshi and his disappearance, and its ominous nature had left me with a terrible premonition.
......Could something that trivial have been the transgression... that summoned Oyashiro-sama's emissary...?
How absurd.
...I could never accept such a thing, and I groaned inwardly... over and over again...
I was hardly the only one bottling up these feelings.
......Mion and Rena were the same.@k Every last one of our classmates was.
Rika-chan knew something about Satoko's situation.@k ...But she wouldn't tell us a thing.
......Rika-chan had a serene, languid air about her, like a cat basking in the sun...@k but once she made up her mind about something, she was unbelievably stubborn about it.
If Rika-chan had decided not to speak, no amount of pressing would change her mind.
...Everyone understood that... so nobody tried to interrogate her.
``...If Rika-chan won't tell us, then all we can do is wait until she's ready.@k@vS03/02/140200003. Besides, maybe the fact that she won't say anything means it's nothing worth worrying about.``
The anxious look on her face robbed those reassuring words of any persuasive power.
Mion sat in sullen silence... gazing up at the sky with a vaguely displeased expression.
...Was she unhappy that Rika-chan wouldn't confide in her own friends...?
...Or was she anxious because she didn't know whether Satoko was safe? I couldn't tell which.
...And what about me?
......The anxiety was suffocating me, day after day.
If someone were to look at me from the outside...... would I look irritated too, just like Mion...?
......Tap, tap.
......Someone tapped my shoulder.
It was Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun.
``...Maebara-san.@k@vS03/24/142400002. Could we talk to you for a moment?``
``Did your ball get stuck again?@k@vS03/01/140100005. Try getting it yourselves this time.@k@vS03/01/140100006. You can't keep relying on your upperclassmen forever...``
I wasn't in the mood to humor the younger kids, and the coldness crept into my reply.
``......There's a bit of a troubling rumor going around.``
changeReason: Japanese 気になる噂 means 'troubling/concerning rumor'; old 'interesting rumor' vs new 'troubling rumor' convey different emotional valences about the information.

TextENGNew: ``...Yeah.@k@vS03/25/142500003. ...We just thought... it might be connected, so...``
A rumor...
......They hadn't said it was about Satoko, but it clearly wasn't unrelated.
They said they didn't want to talk in the classroom and led me behind the school building, which only deepened my conviction...
Behind the school building, it was damp and humid as always.
...Not the kind of place students came to by choice.
Then again, there might not have been a more ideal spot for trading rumors away from prying eyes.
``......Tomita-kun, Okamura-kun.@k@vS03/01/140100009. ...This rumor... it's related to why Satoko has been absent, isn't it...?``
``We don't know for sure if it's related, but... it might be......``
``...What happened to Satoko?@k@vS03/01/140100011. Is she sick?@k@vS03/01/140100012. Or could it be something worse... a serious illness, or an injury, or...``
``No... actually...@k@vS03/24/142400004. it's kind of the opposite...``
``My grandma was saying...@k@vS03/24/142400006. that she came to buy tofu at our shop yesterday around noon.``
``Oh--Tomita's family runs a tofu shop,`` Okamura-kun added.
``......She went to a tofu shop...@k@vS03/01/VT1_keiichi3002. ...to buy tofu......?``
Even I could tell how dumbfounded I sounded.
...Not that I could blame myself.
...I'd been imagining some grave illness or terrible injury... and all the while, the person in question had been casually shopping for tofu.

``So... how did Satoko seem?@k@vS03/01/140100016. Did she look healthy?@k@vS03/01/140100017. Was she wearing a mask like she was recovering from something...?@k@vS03/01/140100018. Or was she on crutches, covered in bandages......?``
``No... there didn't really seem to be... any sign of illness or injury, but...``
If she wasn't ill and she wasn't injured, I should have been able to breathe a sigh of relief.
...But the unease in my chest wouldn't go away.
......Because Tomita-kun and his friend's expressions hadn't brightened one bit.
``She apparently bought one block of silken tofu... but my grandma said something was off about it...``
changeReason: Japanese 絹ごし豆腐 specifically means 'silken tofu'; old 'soft tofu' is less precise than the culturally specific term used in new translation.

TextENGNew: ``Off?@k@vS03/01/140100020. How so?@k@vS03/01/140100021. What's wrong with buying silken tofu?``
changeReason: Japanese 絹ごし is specifically 'silken'; old 'soft' vs new 'silken' affects the specificity of the tofu terminology central to this scene's clue.

TextENGNew: Their roundabout way of speaking irritated me, and I fought desperately to keep the irritation from seeping into my voice.

``Well, Houjou and Furude live on their own, and they're not exactly well-off, right?@k@vS03/24/142400010. ...So they only ever buy the cheap firm tofu.@k@vS03/24/142400011. ...They've never once bought the silken kind--it's the most expensive one we sell.``
``......Firm or silken, it's all tofu, right?@k@vS03/01/140100023. Maybe she just felt like having the good stuff for once.``
``W-Well... maybe, but......``
...Even I knew how hollow I sounded.
......This wasn't trivial--it was some kind of important clue.
Not the cheap firm tofu, but the expensive silken kind.
...Shopping not in the evening after school, but in the middle of the day.
......What those things meant... I couldn't quite piece together.
As I stood there lost in thought, Okamura-kun spoke up.
``...My turn.@k@vS03/25/142500006. ......This is just a rumor too, but... apparently, he's come back.``
``Come back?@k@vS03/01/140100025. What do you mean?@k@vS03/01/140100026. Who came back?``
Satoko's uncle.
......That's right, I think Coach had mentioned him.
...After Satoko and Satoshi lost their parents in an accident, they'd gone to live with their aunt and uncle...
``Houjou's uncle... the one who supposedly fled to the city last year because he was scared of Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS03/25/142500009. ......The guy's terrifying and violent, like a yakuza...``
Everything Coach had told me... came flooding back.
...That's right.
The cruel aunt and uncle who had tormented the siblings endlessly.
...After the aunt was beaten to death by some deranged man, the uncle fled in fear of the curse, drifting between acquaintances' homes in Okinomiya... or so I'd heard.
changeReason: Japanese 知人宅 means 'acquaintances' homes'; old 'lover's house' is a mistranslation that changes the uncle's living situation significantly.

TextENGNew: ``...If that man came back, why would Satoko be skipping school and buying silken tofu in the middle of the day?``
changeReason: Japanese 絹ごし豆腐 is 'silken tofu'; old 'soft tofu' is less precise.

TextENGNew: The two underclassmen fell silent, looking down at the ground.
......But I could tell there was a crucial hint buried in what they'd said.
...Even if I couldn't see it, if I told Mion or Rena, maybe they'd understand...
``Oh... sorry, you two.@k@vS03/01/140100029. ...You went out of your way to tell me, and I ended up grilling you about it...``
``Oh... no, it's fine...``
``At least now we know she isn't bedridden with some terrible illness, right?@k@vS03/25/142500013. That's something, isn't it?``
......That was true, I supposed.
......But... it wouldn't lift.
The dark clouds over my heart... wouldn't lift.
``Ah!!@k@vS03/25/142500015. M-Maebara-san, look over there!!``
Okamura-kun let out a shrill cry, pointing over my shoulder at something behind me.
Startled, I spun around... and far across the schoolyard... near the school gate, I spotted a figure plodding along.
``It's Houjou...!!``
I broke into a run before I could even form words.
...To see with my own eyes, as quickly as possible, that Satoko was all right.
``Sa......Satokoooo!!!``
Satoko seemed startled by me shouting and sprinting toward her.
``Oh my!@k@vS03/04/140400002. Goodness, what is the matter, Keiichi-san...!@k@vS03/04/140400003. Shouting and running at me like that--you gave me quite the fright...!``
``Idiot, never mind about me!!@k@vS03/01/140100032. What happened to you, Satoko--three whole days!!@k@vS03/01/140100033. I was worried sick...!!!``
``Oh-ho-ho-ho...!@k@vS03/04/140400005. Such a worrywart you are.@k@vS03/04/140400006. It was only three days, you know?@k@vS03/04/140400007. Honestly, Keiichi-san, you do worry too much...``
Listening to her words alone... she sounded like the usual Satoko, but something was clearly wrong.
Her smile was fragile somehow...... and her complexion seemed poor.
``Houjou...!``
``Are you okay?! You weren't sick or anything...??``
Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun both rushed to express their concern.
``Just... what happened to you...!?@k@vS03/01/140100035. Three days!@k@vS03/01/140100036. Do you have any idea how worried I was...!?``
``Oh, do forgive me.@k@vS03/04/140400009. I was simply tidying up the house, you see.@k@vS03/04/140400010. It hadn't been used for quite some time, so everything was absolutely covered in dust...``
``...Huh?@k@vS03/01/140100038. ...Satoko, what are you talking about?@k@vS03/01/140100039. Why would you miss school just to clean the house?``
``Honestly.@k@vS03/04/140400012. Is it not about lunchtime?@k@vS03/04/140400013. I am positively famished.``
...It was indeed lunch break.
If she said she was hungry and wanted to eat, I couldn't very well hold her back.

``...Well, fine!@k@vS03/01/140100041. Rika-chan's been bringing your bento every day for three days, you know?@k@vS03/01/140100042. Looks like today you'll actually get to eat it.@k@vS03/01/140100043. Come on, let's get to the classroom!@k@vS03/01/140100044. Everyone'll be so relieved to see you!``
``Indeed.@k@vS03/04/140400015. ...I suppose I may have caused everyone a great deal of worry.``
``...Oh-ho-ho.@k@vS03/04/140400017. It is such a burden being so popular.``
I patted Satoko on the shoulder and we ran toward the classroom together.
......Up close, Satoko did indeed look somehow frail. Her face was flushed as though she had a fever, and her eyes seemed slightly unfocused.
changeReason: New translation adds significant detail about flushed face and unfocused eyes (present in JPN 熱があるかのように顔が赤く、目もどこか、焦点がぼやけている) that was missing from the old, which only mentioned she looked 'somehow weak'.

TextENGNew: ......Seeing Satoko again was supposed to wash away all my anxiety...
That's what I had believed.
..................Instead, new fears welled up inside me.
What on earth... happened to you, Satoko...?
The entire class reacted exactly as I had.
We were worried about you! Glad you're okay! What were you doing for three days?
Our classmates bombarded her with one question after another.
``I truly am sorry for causing such a fuss...! Oh-ho-ho-ho!!``
Laughing with that dry cackle, she devoured the bento Rika-chan had prepared for her.
Word of Satoko's arrival must have reached the staff room, because our teacher came bursting into the classroom, out of breath.
changeReason: Institutional term changed from 'teacher's lounge' to 'staff room' (職員室); both are valid but 'staff room' is the more standard British English term for this Japanese concept.

TextENGNew: ``Houjou-san!!``
``Oh, Sensei.@k@vS03/04/140400020. I do apologize for worrying you these three days!@k@vS03/04/140400021. Oh-ho-ho!@k@vS03/04/140400022. As you can see, I am perfectly fine.``
``......Honestly... I was so worried about you!@k@vS03/22/141400005. When you finish eating, come to the staff room.@k@vS03/22/141400006. We have a lot to discuss.``
changeReason: Institutional term changed from 'teacher's lounge' to 'staff room' (職員室).

TextENGNew: ``Three days of playing hooky--I'm sure I'm in for quite the scolding, oh-ho-ho...!``
Satoko laughed sheepishly and scratched her head, and the whole class erupted into laughter.
The teacher was putting on an angry front, but she breathed a sigh of relief at seeing Satoko laughing and eating her lunch, surrounded by her classmates.
``......What a relief, huh, Rena?``
``Huh?@k@vS03/02/140200005. ......Oh, sorry.@k@vS03/02/140200006. Yeah, I'm glad she's all right.``
......Looking at Rena, I was certain of it.
...Rena, too, sensed that something about Satoko was off.
I glanced over to see Mion standing apart from the commotion as well, her expression somewhat grim.
......But Rika-chan was the opposite--she was beaming from ear to ear.
She kept opening one bento box after another as Satoko emptied them...
Eventually Satoko was full, and after chatting happily with her classmates for a while, she remembered that she'd been summoned to the staff room.
changeReason: Institutional term changed from 'teacher's lounge' to 'staff room' (職員室).

TextENGNew: ``...What a bother, but I suppose she'll be even angrier if I don't go.``
``......I'll go with you.``
When Satoko rose from her seat, Rika-chan silently sidled up alongside her.
changeReason: Japanese ツツツ describes quiet, gliding movement; old 'runs over' implies speed, while new 'silently sidled up' captures the quiet, careful nature of the movement.

TextENGNew: ``Do excuse me, Keiichi-san.@k@vS03/04/140400026. I'll just pop off to receive my scolding.``
``R-Right.@k@vS03/01/140100047. ...I could come along too, if you want.@k@vS03/01/140100048. After the curry incident, we're comrades in getting lectured in the staff room, aren't we?``
changeReason: Institutional term changed from 'teacher's lounge' to 'staff room' (職員室).

TextENGNew: ``......That won't be necessary.@k@vS03/05/140500003. ...Keiichi should stay here and hold down the fort.``
Rika-chan's words were gentle, but her eyes held a clear, firm refusal.
The two of them walked out into the hallway, shoulders pressed close together...
Once they were gone, the class seemed to breathe a collective sigh of relief, scattering back to their usual lunch break routines.
But we alone could not return to normal.
Not me, not Mion, and certainly not Rena.
...We stared at the classroom doorway Satoko and Rika-chan had left through, our expressions distant and severe.
changeReason: Subject changed from 'They stared' to 'We stared'; the narrator is included in the group staring, matching the Japanese 俺 (I) perspective.

TextENGNew: ``......We're friends.@k@vS03/01/140100051. ...All of us. Aren't we?``
My sudden, strange declaration made Mion and Rena turn toward me with puzzled looks.
``I don't keep secrets from my friends.@k@vS03/01/140100053. So let me tell you what I just heard.``

I told them what Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun had shared behind the school building--the tofu shop story, and the rumor that Satoko's uncle had returned.@k I didn't wait for them to respond. I just laid it all out.
``...That's everything I know.@k@vS03/01/140100055. So if either of you knows anything, please tell me.@k@vS03/01/140100056. ...I've held nothing back.``
I put force into my final words and looked into Mion's and Rena's eyes in turn...
Rena met my gaze steadily.
...There wasn't the slightest waver in her eyes.
Without needing to speak, I could tell she had nothing to hide.
......And then, together, our gazes turned to Mion.
``......It's not... like I was deliberately keeping quiet.``
Mion couldn't seem to endure our silent stares... and began to speak haltingly, as though she had something to feel guilty about.
``......But... now that you've spoken up, Kei-chan... I can't exactly stay silent...``
``...Mii-chan... you know something?``
``...Well... I knew from the very first day that Satoko's uncle had come back...@k@vS03/03/140300005. That he... just drifted back into Hinamizawa last Sunday......``
``...Why didn't you say anything?!``
My angry shout made Mion flinch, her gaze dropping... and she hung her head, looking miserable...
``Keiichi-kun, there's nothing to get angry about.@k@vS03/02/140200009. You're scaring Mii-chan.``
``Huh...... oh... sorry, I didn't mean to shout...``
...I bowed my head in apology.
...Mion accepted it, composed herself, and continued.

According to her, Satoko's uncle... had returned because the woman he'd been living with in Okinomiya had vanished, leaving him unable to stay there.@k ...With nowhere else to go, he'd come back to his house in Hinamizawa.
......And then... on that Sunday--the day of the barbecue.
......He and Satoko had come face to face.
``...And now he's forcing her to do all the housework...@k@vS03/03/MIO040102. That's... the rumor, anyway...``
Why the hell didn't you tell us something that important?!
...I nearly screamed it at her, but caught myself just in time...
``M-Mii-chan, is...... is that true?!``
``I haven't seen it with my own eyes... so I don't know if it's true or not...``
Mion dropped her gaze again... and stared at the floor in silence.
``......Just like last year?@k@vS03/02/140200013. Like when they were living with their awful uncle and his wife...?``
``......But... this uncle, he can't do any housework himself, right?@k@vS03/01/KEI040102. ...If he needs Satoko that badly, then maybe he wouldn't necessarily treat her cruelly...?``
``...Keiichi-kun, I don't know what you're basing that on.@k@vS03/02/140200015. You shouldn't say such irresponsible things.``
Rena scolded me plainly and directly.
...Don't speak as if you know what you're talking about when you clearly don't. That was her unmistakable message...
``...Then... do you... do you know something, Rena......?``
``......I don't know anything either.@k@vS03/02/140200018. ...But listen.@k@vS03/02/140200019. ......Things are completely different from a year ago.``
``...Different... how?``
``Satoshi-kun isn't here.``

``......Satoshi... was a wonderful brother who truly cared about his little sister.@k@vS03/03/140300015. ...He always protected Satoko.@k@vS03/03/140300016. ......He made himself a shield for her... so that as little as possible of the aunt and uncle's cruelty would reach her.``
``......The uncle and Satoko-chan are alone in that house now, just the two of them.@k@vS03/02/140200022. ......Can't you imagine how suffocating that must be, Keiichi-kun?``

``He only came back because the woman he was living with vanished and he couldn't fend for himself, right?@k@vS03/02/140200024. ......Do you really think someone who came back for such a bitter reason would treat Satoko-chan kindly?``
``......Oh...... no, I'm sorry too.``
``...I was starting to sound like I was interrogating you, wasn't I? Hau~!``
An awkward silence settled over us.
......The three of us sat without a word... just staring at the floor...
Before long, we heard the principal ringing his handbell.
The teacher returned with Satoko and Rika-chan in tow.
``All right, everyone, afternoon classes are starting!@k@vS03/22/141400008. Class president, the call!``
``Everybody riiiiise!!``
``Oh-hohoho... my apologies.@k@vS03/04/140400028. Excuse me, excuse me...``
Satoko's vaguely sheepish expression was impossible to read.
...But the clouded look on Rika-chan's face behind her...@k made it very hard to laugh off the disquieting things Mion had told us......
``All riiiight!!@k@vS03/03/140300019. It's been ages since the whole gang's been together!!@k@vS03/03/140300020. Let's go all out and have an epic club battle!!``
Yeah, bring it on!!
Of course... at times like these, the best thing to do was throw ourselves into the fierce scheming and chaos of a club activity!!
``Ehehehehe!@k@vS03/02/140200028. Now we're talking~!@k@vS03/02/140200029. Hey, Satoko-chan!@k@vS03/02/140200030. Let's play, let's play!``
``There was that game you liked, right? What was it again...@k@vS03/03/140300022. Any game is fine!@k@vS03/03/140300023. Go on, name it!@k@vS03/03/140300024. Name it~!!``
Mion laughed cheerfully as she pulled game after game from the club locker and lined them up on the desk.
I don't think Mion had ever let someone else choose the game before.
......It was obvious she was being considerate of Satoko.
...Whatever her circumstances, if we could all just spend some fun time together... maybe that would be some small comfort...
Satoko seemed to appreciate the sentiment...... and smiled, vaguely but warmly.
......But... she slowly shook her head.
``I'm ever so grateful, but... the thought alone is more than enough.@k@vS03/04/140400030. ......There are... various things I must attend to, you see...``
......Even Rika-chan bowed her head with an apologetic expression.

``Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa--seriously?!@k@vS03/03/140300026. The full roster's finally here and we're not going to play?!@k@vS03/03/140300027. Deserting before battle earns a punishment game--what article of the club rules was that again?!``
``...Stop it, Mii-chan.@k@vS03/02/140200032. ...We can't force her to stay.``
``...Hey, this old man isn't forcing anyone...!@k@vS03/03/140300029. I just thought it'd be way more fun if we all went wild together...!``

``...Truly, just your feelings alone make me happy.@k@vS03/04/140400032. ...Rest assured--next time we meet in battle, I shall ensnare every last one of you in a magnificent trap!@k@vS03/04/140400033. ...Oh-ho-ho-ho!``

Even as she traded barbs with us, Satoko apologized for not being able to stay for club activities...@k She quickly gathered her things into her bag and pattered out into the hallway, vanishing from sight...
It all happened in the blink of an eye. ......Her face had looked slightly flushed, as though she had a fever, and that had been nagging at me--but I never got the chance to bring it up.
changeReason: New translation restores significant descriptive detail about Satoko's flushed face and slightly dazed look that was entirely absent from the terse old translation.

TextENGNew: Rika-chan watched forlornly as her best friend hurried home, unable to even stay for club...
The classroom fell deathly silent.
......The lively voices of our classmates in the schoolyard and the droning of the cicadas seemed to come from some distant world.
...Mion broke the silence with that bitter remark as she began putting the games back into the locker.
......Everyone else just stared blankly at Satoko's now-empty seat.
``...Rika-chan.@k@vS03/02/140200034. Don't you think it's about time you told us?``
Rena put on the gentlest smile she could muster and spoke softly to Rika-chan.
......Even without hearing it from Rika-chan, we had a fairly clear picture of the situation by now.
Satoko's uncle had returned to Hinamizawa after his companion disappeared.
Assuming that was common knowledge, I skipped straight to the point.
...Perhaps Mion and Rena... wanted to ask the very same question.
...No one interjected--we all simply waited in silence for Rika-chan's answer.
Rika-chan still wouldn't answer.
......We'd been hoping for a denial... but her silence was answer enough, and a bitter one at that.
``......Last year, was it?@k@vS03/01/140100068. It was like this when the uncle and his wife were both there, right?``

``...Yeah.@k@vS03/03/140300033. Back then, most of it was collateral damage from their fights as a couple.@k@vS03/03/140300034. ...One of the things we could do was keep her busy with club activities so she'd spend as little time at home as possible, but...``
...Damn it...
Satoko is still just a child...!
How dare he ignore his own inability to take care of himself and treat her like a servant...!

``...When you think about it rationally, isn't this clearly wrong?@k@vS03/01/KEI040104. I know we'd need circumstantial evidence, but this has to be a real problem, right?@k@vS03/01/140100071. Can't we report it to the police or something?``
``...You're right.@k@vS03/02/140200036. ...If we had evidence, we could report it to the police.@k@vS03/02/140200037. ...Or maybe we could consult the Child Welfare Center in Okinomiya about it?``
Rena and I kept tossing around words like 'police' as though it were simple, and Mion's response carried a hint of condescension.
``...It's easy for you two to say that.@k@vS03/03/140300037. But have you considered the risks of actually trying to prove it?``
``Risks... what do you mean?``

``...It was two winters ago, I think.@k@vS03/03/140300039. ...We reported it.@k@vS03/03/MIO040103. To the Child Welfare Center in Okinomiya.@k@vS03/03/MIO040104. We told them there was a problem and begged them to do something immediately.``
``Huh... you did?@k@vS03/02/140200039. ...What happened?``

``A caseworker came right away after we called.@k@vS03/03/140300043. ...She interviewed Satoko and Satoshi about their situation.@k@vS03/03/140300044. And the aunt and uncle too, while she was at it.``
``...Interviewing Satoko and Satoshi is one thing, but... wasn't it risky to talk to the aunt and uncle directly...?``
``They said they needed to hear both sides before making a comprehensive judgment.``
``...And... what was the result?``
``Wait and see.``
``...'Wait and see'... what the hell does that even mean?!``
``Then... what happened after that...?``

``For a while, a caseworker visited on the same day every week, apparently.@k@vS03/03/MIO040107. The uncle and his wife knew they were being watched, so they toned down their behavior.@k@vS03/03/140300058. ......But obviously... that wasn't going to solve anything.``
Mion sighed and nodded.

``......The aunt was especially persistent.@k@vS03/03/140300060. ...That insidious cruelty some women have, you know?@k@vS03/03/140300061. ......Apparently she did some truly vile things.@k@vS03/03/140300062. ......Satoko... by the end, she was like a ragdoll that only knew how to breathe...``
...So what does that mean?
......In the end... did it turn out that reporting it had only made things worse...?
``...Do you understand now?@k@vS03/03/MIO040108. Without proof so clear that anyone can see it, we're helpless.@k@vS03/03/140300065. ...Reporting it could even make things worse.``
``......That whole thing... was a mistake...``
......Once a report is filed, it absolutely has to result in a guilty verdict.
......To avoid repeating last year's blunder... we might need far more ironclad evidence......
``......It's just... sad, somehow.@k@vS03/02/140200041_2. ...So what you're saying is... we have to just sit here and watch in silence... right...?``
......I realized with a start that my own cautious plan amounted to nothing more than 'wait and see'......
``......Sorry for bringing this up out of nowhere, but...@k@vS03/01/140100076. ......Rika-chan, how are you able to live on your own...?``
Rika-chan had lost both her parents, if I remembered correctly.
...From what I'd heard, she had no relatives either.

......If we could somehow give Satoko that same freedom...

``...I don't mean it as an insult.@k@vS03/01/140100080. ...I just thought there might be a clue in it--something that could help save Satoko.@k@vS03/01/140100081. ...Rika-chan, I'm sorry if that was insensitive.``

``Then... couldn't the village chief take in Satoko too?!``
We just need to give Satoko a safe place to stay!

``It's not that simple!@k@vS03/03/140300095. Satoko is a growing girl, you know!@k@vS03/03/140300096. Asking someone to take care of a real person isn't like dropping off a kitten!!``

``Then what about Coach?!@k@vS03/01/140100095. How about Coach?!@k@vS03/01/140100096. Coach is... well, I don't know the details, but he's wealthy, right?@k@vS03/01/140100097. He even said so himself!``
``Coach would... I'm sure he'd take wonderful care of her!``

``I said stop it!!@k@vS03/03/140300098. Stop treating Satoko like some kind of pet!!@k@vS03/03/140300099. All you've been doing is talking about pawning her off on someone!@k@vS03/03/140300100. Think more carefully about what it means to take responsibility for another human being's life!!``
``Did you actually ask him?!@k@vS03/03/140300102. Did you get a real commitment from him that he'd take Satoko in?!``
``...N-No, I didn't.@k@vS03/01/140100101. ...But Coach would definitely say yes...!``

``Hah! Fine, let's say he does.@k@vS03/03/140300104. Even granting the impossible that Coach agrees?@k@vS03/03/140300105. It still won't work!!@k@vS03/03/140300106. A single man can't become a legal guardian!!``
That's right...
Coach... he said it himself, didn't he?
...He was single... so he couldn't...!!
D-Damn it!! What the hell is wrong with everything?!
Is being single really that much of a disadvantage in this damn society?!
......Then... then there's no choice but a married, wealthy household!
``...Is there anyone that wealthy in Hinamizawa...?@k@vS03/02/140200054. ...That seems difficult...``

``See?@k@vS03/03/140300108. It's not simple!!@k@vS03/03/140300109. Taking in another person!!@k@vS03/03/140300110. This shallow idea of just shoving her onto someone else is fundamentally flawed!!@k@vS03/03/140300111. You're not thinking this through, Kei-chan!!``

``What the hell is wrong with all of you?!@k@vS03/01/140100103. I'm desperately trying to figure out how to save Satoko, and all you can say is no, no, no!!@k@vS03/01/140100104. Do you even give a damn about Satoko?!``
``Do you honestly not care about your own friend?!@k@vS03/01/140100106. Answer me!!``
``That's...!@k@vS03/03/140300113. ......That's not... what I meant!!@k@vS03/03/140300114. But... it's just not that simple, and...!!``
``It's not complicated!@k@vS03/01/140100108. We just need some well-off family to look after Satoko for a while--that's all there is to it!``

``Right... what about your family, Mion?!@k@vS03/01/140100110. You're always bragging about being one of the Three Great Families, aren't you?!@k@vS03/01/140100111. From what I hear, you live in a massive estate!!``
``M-My house...?!@k@vS03/03/140300116. Absolutely not!! ...My... grandmother would never allow it...!``

``Then what about your parents in Okinomiya?!@k@vS03/01/140100113. I heard they're living in a mansion too!!@k@vS03/01/140100114. And they've got shops all over town run by relatives!!@k@vS03/01/140100115. Your family's loaded, aren't they?!``
``N-No... that's even worse!!@k@vS03/03/140300118. Okinomiya is completely out of the question!!@k@vS03/03/140300119. My father... he would never, ever permit it!!!``

``Oh, come on!!@k@vS03/01/140100117. You're always playing the big shot, but when it actually matters, you shrink back?!@k@vS03/01/140100118. What the hell?!@k@vS03/01/140100119. Are you just going to leave Satoko to suffer?!?!``
``T-That's... I don't want that, but...!!@k@vS03/03/140300121. Of course... I want to help Satoko too!@k@vS03/03/140300122. But... that's a completely different... issue...``

``A different issue?!@k@vS03/01/140100121. You heartless bastard!!@k@vS03/01/140100122. Our friend is in crisis!!@k@vS03/01/140100123. If you won't save her now, then what good are you?!@k@vS03/01/140100124. You're the president!!@k@vS03/01/140100125. The club president!!@k@vS03/01/140100126. Save her!!@k@vS03/01/140100127. Save your friend!!``

``If you won't save her right here, right now, you're no friend--you're not even human!!@k@vS03/01/140100129. Your very humanity is on the line here!!@k@vS03/01/140100130. Are you even listening to me, Mion?!?!``
I didn't give Mion a single opening to fight back... I let every last one of my emotions explode and hurled them at her.
...I just wanted to force Mion to agree--by any means necessary.@k ...If she did, Satoko's problems would be solved.
If pushing was all it took to solve this... then I'd push with everything I had!!
``Why have you gone quiet?!@k@vS03/01/140100132. I'm talking to you!@k@vS03/01/140100133. Well?! Say something, Mion Sonozaki!!!``
...The grim-faced Mion, who had been staring at the floor... began to let out choked sobs...
......Her fists trembled at her sides, clenched tight... and from her tightly shut eyes... tears spilled forth...
......Had I... really been that cruel to her...?
This wasn't the time for tears--this was the crucible where we had to forge a plan to save Satoko!
......Just as I was thinking that, Rena spoke in a terribly calm, cold voice.

``.........Keiichi-kun.@k@vS03/02/140200056. If Mii-chan's family won't work, why not try another wealthy household?@k@vS03/02/140200057. I know someone in Hinamizawa who lives in quite the splendid mansion.``
``Don't play dumb.@k@vS03/02/140200059. You live in that grand house yourself, and you're leaving your own family out of the discussion?``

``She's your friend, isn't she? Then save her yourself, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS03/02/140200061. Mii-chan said no because she's cold-hearted, right?@k@vS03/02/140200062. Then you, the one who cares so much about friends, need to set the example.@k@vS03/02/140200063. Just three of you living in that big house, wasn't it?``

``Then you must have plenty of spare rooms.@k@vS03/02/140200065. Just give a few of those empty rooms to Satoko-chan.@k@vS03/02/140200066. And there we go--Satoko-chan's problems are solved! How about that?``

``Is that it? Are we done?@k@vS03/02/140200068. That was easy, wasn't it!@k@vS03/02/140200069. Great, wonderful, problem solved!!@k@vS03/02/140200070. So we're done for today, right?!@k@vS03/02/140200071. I'm heading home, la la la!!@k@vS03/02/140200072. Maybe I'll go treasure hunting--haven't done that in ages!!``
``I was so worried about Satoko-chan that I haven't felt like playing at all until now!!@k@vS03/02/140200074. I wonder what kind of treasure I'll find today, hauu...``
``............Well?! Say something!@k@vS03/02/140200076. Am I the only one talking here?!``
``Don't just stand there--are you listening, Keiichi Maebara?!?!``
............Even the cicadas... seemed to cower.
Rena, heaving with each breath--and me, who had forgotten how to breathe entirely.
...Mion, her face drained of all color, her mind gone blank--and Rika-chan, sitting in silence as if none of this concerned her.
...The air in the classroom solidified around us... encasing us like concrete.
Clatter.
The sound of Rena's chair as she sat back down... finally gave everyone permission to breathe again.
...Rena... had shown me... exactly how badly I had hurt Mion.
``.........Rena is scary.``
After a moment...@k Rika-chan's words cracked the frozen silence.
``...Yeah.@k@vS03/02/140200079. I'm sorry, Rika-chan.@k@vS03/02/140200080. Mii-chan, I'm sorry too.``
``...I don't need to apologize to Keiichi-kun, do I?``
``...............Yeah.@k@vS03/01/140100139. ...I'm sorry.@k@vS03/01/140100140. ...I was out of line. I said terrible things.``
``Was that all directed at me?@k@vS03/02/140200083. You were glaring at Mii-chan while you said it, so I was certain you were talking to her.``
......Rena possessed a strength... utterly unimaginable from her usual demeanor.
......Someone who just spewed venom in a burst of rage, like me... was no match for her.
Looking back, I couldn't even understand what had gotten me so worked up...
......I considered myself Satoko's Nii-nii...@k so with nothing but good intentions, I'd tried to think harder about her situation than anyone else... and yet.

``Mm...... mm.@k@vS03/03/140300126. ...It's okay.@k@vS03/03/140300127. ...You were agonizing over this with everything you had, and all I did was shoot down every idea...@k@vS03/03/140300128. ...I'm sorry.@k@vS03/03/140300129. I... was wrong too.``
Mion wiped the tears pooling in her eyes with her fists and offered her own apology...
We bowed our heads to each other... unable to meet each other's eyes.
A sigh escaped from someone... I couldn't tell who.
...This situation with Satoko... had been going on since last year.
How had... last year's tragedy rescued Satoko in the end?
How had that chapter been brought to a close...?
......Right.
......In the end... no one could do a thing.
...All they could do was endure... and wait for those days of suffering to end on their own.
...That's right.
The one who had put an end to those terrible days... was some unknown lunatic, strung out on drugs.
On the night of Watanagashi last year... the cruel aunt had been bludgeoned to death.
...But that hadn't been done to save Satoko or Satoshi.
...Right.@k ...It was nothing more than... sheer coincidence.
Her friends--the ones who should have done something for Satoko more than anyone--had been powerless.
...The incident itself may have been a tragedy...@k but from Satoko's perspective...@k it was nothing short of a miracle.
Yes--a miracle.
......A miracle one could only sit and wait for.
``......Is waiting really all...@k@vS03/01/140100145. we can do?``
And I didn't mean some trivial thing like waiting for evidence of abuse.
``......A miracle.``
...Nobody mocked me for using such an outlandish word.
......Because when we couldn't think of a single plan... it was the only thing left to hope for...
That single word Mion let fall... was the final blow.
......It was hot.
......My whole body was sticky with unpleasant sweat.
...The worst... June.
